title: Reliability Scan
findings: 发现 3 个高信号 reliability 问题：store counters 在损坏/迁移中会 fail-open 导致重复 stable_id，store/global/project config 的读改写缺少锁会丢失并发更新。

issues_found.title: Corrupt store counters fail open to zero and can re-mint existing stable_ids
issues_found.description: readStoreCounters returns default zero counters when counters.json is unreadable, invalid JSON, or schema-invalid, and allocateStoreKnowledgeId immediately uses that value inside the lock before writing nextCounters. If counters.json is corrupted while knowledge files with higher IDs still exist, the next approval can mint KT/KP IDs that already exist, corrupting cite identity and file naming invariants.
issues_found.fix_direction: Make allocation fail closed on corrupt counters.json, preserve a .corrupted sidecar, and require/perform reconcileStoreCounters before minting. Only return zero counters for an absent file, not for parse/schema corruption on the write path.

issues_found.title: store migrate removes sources before counters are reconciled, leaving an interruption window for duplicate IDs
issues_found.description: store-migrate writes imported entries into target stores, then removes the project sources, and only afterwards calls reconcileStoreCounters. Any crash, process kill, disk error, or interrupted command between the target writes/source removal and counter reconciliation leaves the store containing imported stable_ids while counters.json remains stale, so the next allocateStoreKnowledgeId can re-mint those IDs.
issues_found.fix_direction: Make migration transactional: reconcile/atomically write counters for each target before committing/removing sources, or write a migration receipt and recover/reconcile on the next run before any allocation. Use atomic writes and fail closed if counter reconciliation cannot complete.

issues_found.title: Global/project store config mutations use unlocked read-modify-write and can lose concurrent updates
issues_found.description: storeAdd/storeCreate/storeRemove and storeBind/storeSwitchWrite load config, mutate in memory, and save with plain writeFileSync through saveGlobalConfig/saveProjectConfig. Two concurrent CLI operations against the same global or project config can both read the same base state and the later save overwrites the earlier mount/bind/write-target update; interruption during write can also leave a truncated config that later loadGlobalConfig/loadProjectConfig parses without recovery.
issues_found.fix_direction: Wrap global/project config read-modify-write operations in withFileLock keyed by the config path and persist via atomicWriteJson with a corruption sidecar/recovery path. Consider helper APIs that combine load-mutate-save under one lock.
